title: "Complete Other Alias": Rep. Luna Drops Clinton-Epstein Bombshell
summary:

            Authored by Steve Watson via modernity.news,

Rep. Anna Paulina Luna appeared on Bill Maher’s show and confirmed what the Epstein document dumps have long hinted at: the former president wasn’t just flying on the Lolita Express — he was operating under an entirely different identity in the files.

President Trump’s decision to join Israel in launching a regime-change war on Iran has so far cost the lives of at least 13 American service members. More than 200 have been wounded, dozens seriously enough to require evacuations to military hospitals in Europe and the United States. title: "Going To Cripple Our Economy": Small Businesses Sound Alarm Over Record Diesel Price Spike
summary:
"Going To Cripple Our Economy": Small Businesses Sound Alarm Over Record Diesel Price Spike

            The latest AAA fuel data from across America shows that the national average diesel price at the pump has jumped nearly 40% this month, surpassing the 2022 fuel spike that followed Russia's invasion of Ukraine.



Surging diesel prices are already generating a shock across trucking, rail, shipping, farm equipment, construction machinery, generators, and much of industrial logistics, given tha

title: The Oscars Died Last Weekend...Did Anyone Even Notice?
summary:
The Oscars Died Last Weekend...Did Anyone Even Notice?

            Submitted by QTR's Fringe Finance

“Oh, the Oscars was yesterday,” my friend said to me while walking through town. She noticed that a nearby bar had the TV tuned to a replay of the ceremony.

What surprised me was who said it. This is a person who is deeply, spiritually committed to things like what movies are in theaters and new fictional television shows. She loves the arts. She listens to jazz. She goes to see Broadway plays

title: New York City Is Spending $81,000 Per Year On Each Homeless Person
summary:
New York City Is Spending $81,000 Per Year On Each Homeless Person

            New York City spent about $368 million last year on services for people living on the streets, which equals roughly $81,000 per unsheltered person, according to the NY Post.

Spending through the city’s New York City Department of Homeless Services street outreach programs has increased sharply over the past several years. In 2019, the city spent about $102 million on these services, averaging around $28,000 per unsh

title: The State Will Always Socialize The Cost Of War
summary:
The State Will Always Socialize The Cost Of War

            Via The Libertarian Institute

War is often sold to the public as an act of national will: decisive, necessary, and under control. The bill arrives later, in a quieter form. It shows up in insurance markets, shipping rates, emergency guarantees, higher fuel prices, and sudden policy reversals designed to keep the economic damage from spreading too far or too fast. That is what is now happening with the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran. The fig

summary:
Iran's First Use Of ICBMs Raises Serious Questions About Remaining Arsenal

title: On The Hidden Fragility Of Our Energy-Dependent World & The Cascading Consequences Of A Supply-Shock That Money Alone Can't Fix
summary:

For a long time, I accepted the same framework most people in finance operate within—that the global economy is, at its core, a system governed by monetary policy, shaped by interest rates, and stabilized by central banks. title: STAT+: Automatic enrollment in Medicare Advantage plans under consideration, top Trump health official says
summary: The idea could be a major win for the health insurance industry, which has seen a crackdown on Medicare Advantage in recent years.

title: STAT+: Iran war has not disrupted pharma supply chains. That could change if conflict is prolonged
summary: The escalating war in the Middle East so far has not appreciably disrupted global pharmaceutical supply chains, but it could eventually — affecting prices.

title: STAT+: Sepsis hospitalizations have tripled in Massachusetts. Is it real or a billing game?
summary: In Massachusetts, sepsis cases has more than tripled since 2010. Some blame hospitals' use of AI tools for medical coding and billing.

title: STAT+: Inside the delicate, high-stakes search for a new CDC director
summary: At #STATBreakthrough, top HHS official Chris Klomp talked to STAT about the delicate, high-stakes search for a new CDC director.

title: STAT+: ACIP conflict is test of Trump’s support for RFK Jr.’s vaccine policy overhaul
summary: The Trump administration is weighing how to respond to a court ruling on vaccine policymaking, a decision that could come with political risks.

title: STAT+: Medicare chief says TrumpRx is meant to be narrow in scope
summary: Today's biotech news includes the Medicare director offering a tempered view of TrumpRx and high-dose Wegovy getting an FDA nod.

title: STAT+: Up and down the ladder: The latest comings and goings
summary: From new hires to departures and promotions, here are the latest comings and goings in the pharmaceutical industry.

title: A conversation with the ‘godfather’ of biotech
summary: This week on "The Readout LOUD," a conversation with biotech "godfather" Stelios Papadapolous live from #STATBreakthrough.

summary: In today's Morning Rounds newsletter, the highlights from #STATBreakthrough, a ruling against Kennedy’s gender-affirming care declaration, and more.

summary: And so, another working week will soon come to a close. Not a moment too soon, yes? This is, you may recall, our treasured signal to daydream about weekend plans.…

title: Earendil Labs, an AI-powered drugmaker, hauls in $787M
summary: The hefty funding will support a U.S. and China-based startup that’s amassed a pipeline of nearly 20 drugs and formed two lucrative partnerships with Sanofi.

title: Roche stops work on experimental SMA drug
summary: In a letter to European patients, Roche said its decision to end development of “emugrobart” was based on the drug “not consistently” improving muscle growth and motor function in a key study.

title: Novartis pays $2B to land a startup’s breast cancer drug
summary: Acquiring a unit of privately held Synnovation Therapeutics hands Novartis a prospect that might improve on medicines that, like its own Piqray, target tumors driven to growth by PIK3CA mutations.

title: Rhythm obesity drug wins broader use from FDA
summary: Imcivree’s approval in a type of obesity driven by brain injury could unlock an opportunity Wall Street analysts believe to be worth more than $1 billion.

title: Novo uses FDA voucher to win speedy approval of higher-dose Wegovy
summary: The clearance, issued 54 days after an approval submission, gives Novo another chance to claw back market share from Eli Lilly.

title: Lilly’s three-pronged obesity drug hits goal in large diabetes trial
summary: Retatrutide, a possible advance on GLP-1 drugs like Zepbound, significantly cut blood sugar and body weight in the study, a sign it might be similarly impactful against obesity.

title: Pfizer aims for wider use of PARP drug in prostate cancer with new data
summary: While sales of Talzenna, which Pfizer acquired in a $14 billion deal, have been “negligible” so far, the new results should justify use in earlier lines of care, one analyst said.

title: A biotech VC sees early signs of a turnaround for startups
summary: After a lengthy downturn, renewed optimism is building among young drugmakers and their backers as a new funding wave takes shape, said Santé Ventures’ Omar Khalil.

title: AstraZeneca to boost cell therapy capabilities with new China investment
summary: The planned Shanghai facility extends a yearslong push into the field and will support CAR-T therapies AstraZeneca is making for autoimmune disease and cancer.

title: RBC: Revolution, Xenon, Arrowhead among top takeover targets
summary: As part of a larger review, analyst Leonid Timashev identified 13 companies that could see deal interest as well as more than half a dozen poised to be potential buyers.

title: Genentech antibody fails to boost muscle in rare diseases, raising questions for obesity trial
summary: Genentech is halting development of an antibody for two rare genetic diseases after the candidate failed to boost muscle growth, raising questions about whether the molecule can preserve muscle in two still-ongoing obesity trials.

title: Intuitive recalls surgical stapler reloads following patient death
summary: This comes in the same week that Intuitive reported a cyberattack, with a phishing incident targeting some of its employees.

title: Behind the curtain: What Xaira is building after its $1B fundraise
summary: What has Xaira been doing since its $1 billion raise? Xaira COO Jeff Jonker joins "The Top Line" to talk strategy, AI and the company’s long-game vision for drug discovery.

title: Chutes & Ladders—Eric Topol boards Flagship as advisor
summary: Renowned cardiologist Eric Topol, M.D., is setting sail with Flagship Pioneering as an academic advisor for the firm’s Preemptive Health and Medicine Initiative. Topol is director of the Scripps Research Translational Institute, which he founded in 2007 within San Diego biomedicine hub Scripps Research.

title: Novartis pays Synnovation $2B upfront for breast cancer program as rivals circle
summary: Novartis has struck a deal to buy Synnovation Therapeutics’ pan-mutant‑selective PI3Kα inhibitor for $2 billion upfront, securing an asset that could defend its breast cancer franchise from challengers including Eli Lilly.

title: Gilead turns down chance to build on Assembly's phase 1-stage HBV antiviral
summary: Gilead has handed back a next-gen hepatitis B antiviral to Assembly Biosciences, while the two companies continue to partner on other assets.

title: Verily banks $300M to accelerate AI road map, transitions to independent company
summary: Verily raised a $300 million investment round to accelerate its work in artificial intelligence and precision health as it also moves out from under the corporate umbrella of Alphabet.

title: Embecta to buy auto-injector maker Owen Mumford for $200M to diversify beyond insulin
summary: Embecta has agreed to pay 100 million pounds sterling upfront for the company, or about $134.3 million, plus another 50 million pounds in sales milestones.

title: Genentech shreds RIPK1 inhibitor from pipeline after early ph. 2 failure
summary: Genentech has elected to stop a phase 2 trial of a RIPK1 inhibitor early and dump the asset after an analysis revealed the candidate was unlikely to meet the study’s primary endpoint.

title: Pfizer adds to Seagen scrap heap with another early cancer candidate cull
summary: About a month after Pfizer CEO Albert Bourla, Ph.D., told investors that the Big Pharma’s pipeline pruning was mostly done, the company has trimmed another branch. Pfizer has scrapped a phase 1 antibody-drug conjugate that was being tested in solid tumors, a company spokesperson confirmed to Fierce Biotech.

summary:
Roche's subsidiary Genentech has called off the late-stage development of a muscle-building drug in two rare genetic conditions, spinal muscular atrophy (SMA) and facioscapulohumeral muscular dystrophy (FSHD).

title: How to Boost Trust in Tech? 4 Perspectives from SXSW
summary:
Here are four perspectives from providers, patients, life science executives and healthcare technologists about ways to boost trust at a time of rapid AI deployments.
The post How to Boost Trust in Tech? 4 Perspectives from SXSW appeared first on MedCity News.

title: Why Providence Wants to Sell Its Health Plan
summary:
Providence announced its intent to sell its health plan, exiting the “payvider” model as rising costs and operational complexity weigh on smaller regional insurers. The move reflects a broader trend of health systems refocusing on core care delivery and exploring partnerships instead of running insurance themselves.
The post Why Providence Wants to Sell Its Health Plan appeared first on MedCity News.

title: Lantern Secures $30M to Fuel Growth Across Employers and Health Plans
summary:
Lantern’s $30 million raise was led by Morgan Health, a JPMorganChase business unit focused on employer-sponsored healthcare, as well as Echo Health Ventures, a strategic investment platform that invests on behalf of multiple Blues health plans.
The post Lantern Secures $30M to Fuel Growth Across Employers and Health Plans appeared first on MedCity News.

title: AI, Discernment, and the Discipline of Access to Healthcare
summary:
The systems getting value from AI aren’t doing more, they’re choosing better. Instead of chasing ten initiatives, they align around the few levers that actually move the enterprise. 
The post AI, Discernment, and the Discipline of Access to Healthcare appeared first on MedCity News.

title: Ending the Voicemail Bottleneck: How Voice AI Is Transforming Patient Access
summary:
Beyond simply recording or transcribing messages to lower call volumes, truly autonomous voice AI puts patients in the driver’s seat of their own healthcare. It processes patient requests and information immediately and integrates actionable insights into the EHR, speeding next steps for clinicians and patients. 
The post Ending the Voicemail Bottleneck: How Voice AI Is Transforming Patient Access appeared first on MedCity News.

title: Inside One VC’s Litmus Test for Investable Healthcare AI Startups
summary:
Katie Jacobs Stanton, founder of Moxxie Ventures, shared some details of how she and her team evaluate healthcare AI startups. She stressed the need for founders with deep industry expertise, differentiated products and realistic distribution strategies.
The post Inside One VC’s Litmus Test for Investable Healthcare AI Startups appeared first on MedCity News.

title: How PreSeed Is Tackling the Sperm Donor Shortage
summary:
A new startup called PreSeed Fertility launched on Thursday. The company is a recruitment and matching platform for sperm donors.

title: Behavior Changes Happen Outside the Exam Room, But Validation of Lifestyle Medicine Programs Cannot
summary:
If CMS and private payers are serious about whole-person health, reimbursement structures must align with the workforce actually delivering that care. Payment pathways should recognize the value of the broader care team and enable the seamless integration of their expertise with the objective data generated through connected devices and programs like RPM.
The post Behavior Changes Happen Outside the Exam Room, But Validation of Lifestyle Medicine Programs Cannot appeared first on MedCity News.

title: When Oral Health Joins the Care Team
summary:
By strengthening the connection between dentists and physicians, we can enable real-time care coordination, ensure patients get follow-up when needed and help teams spot risk factors earlier.
